title: Committees, Inquiries, and Royal Commissions
shortSummary: Understand the scrutiny machinery around politics: parliamentary committees, public inquiries, integrity processes, and royal commissions.
whyItMatters: A political system is only as trustworthy as its correction mechanisms. Committees, inquiries, audits, regulators, and royal commissions are where claims get tested and failures become visible.
coreConcepts: Why scrutiny is slow on purpose
mentalModels: Not every failure goes straight to a royal commission
Scrutiny is the corrective layer around power
Recommendations still have to re-enter politics to matter

goDeeper.label: Analyse a royal commission
goDeeper.description: Trace one major inquiry from trigger through findings to reform.

goDeeper.label: See the committee secretariat day-in-the-life
goDeeper.description: Ground scrutiny work in a real operational role.
